prevalence.source: Endocrinology epidemiology syntheses; Endocrine Society of India context
prevalence.trend: stable; increasing detection with routine calcium testing

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; morbidity from stones and bone loss

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Elevated calcium with inappropriately high PTH; assess vitamin D, renal function and bone density

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Lab testing in urban centers; parathyroid imaging in tertiary hospitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Parathyroidectomy for symptomatic or meeting criteria; monitoring for mild cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited surgical access and delayed referral in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear diagnostic criteria and effective surgery when available
